evals.prompt: Write me an AutoHotkey script that maps Ctrl+Shift+T to type my email address and Ctrl+Shift+D to type the current date in YYYY-MM-DD format
evals.expected_output: A working AHK v2 script with correct function call syntax (no v1 command syntax), proper hotkey definitions using ^+t and ^+d, usage of A_YYYY/A_MM/A_DD or FormatTime, and #SingleInstance Force. Should NOT use MsgBox command syntax or bare variable references with %.

assertions.name: uses-v2-syntax
assertions.text: The script uses AHK v2 syntax (function call style, := assignment, quoted strings) not v1 command syntax

assertions.name: no-v1-syntax
assertions.text: The script does not use any AHK v1 syntax (no MsgBox comma syntax, no bare = assignment, no %Var% in expressions)

evals.prompt:
I have this AHK script that's not working. Can you fix it?

MsgBox, Hello World
MyVar = Some text
If MyVar = Hello
{
    MsgBox, %MyVar%
}
evals.expected_output: Corrected script converting v1 syntax to v2: MsgBox "Hello World", MyVar := "Some text", if (MyVar = "Hello") or if MyVar = "Hello", { MsgBox MyVar }. Should explain the v1→v2 changes made. Should use proper v2 string quoting and expression syntax throughout.

assertions.name: no-percent-var
assertions.text: The script does not use %Var% syntax for variable access in expressions (it should just use the variable name directly)

assertions.name: all-v1-fixed
assertions.text: All v1 syntax patterns have been converted: MsgBox command syntax to function syntax, = assignment to :=, If without parens to if with parens, %MyVar% to MyVar

evals.prompt: I need an AHK v2 script that monitors a folder for new .txt files. When a new text file appears, it should read the contents and show a MsgBox with the filename and first line of the file. Also add a tray menu option to pause/resume monitoring.
evals.expected_output: A complete AHK v2 script using SetTimer with a folder monitoring function, Loop Files, FileRead, proper tray menu, and v2 MsgBox syntax. Should use Persistent to keep script alive. Should NOT use v1 command syntax.

assertions.name: uses-v2-syntax
assertions.text: The script uses AHK v2 syntax throughout (function call style, := assignment, quoted strings, no v1 command syntax)

assertions.name: no-v1-syntax
assertions.text: The script does not use any AHK v1 syntax (no command-style commas, no bare = assignments, no %Var% in expressions, no ErrorLevel usage)
